expected: After a successful LiveKit join, unmount/refresh/navigation cleanup records participantState left exactly once.
Explicit leave followed by unmount does not duplicate left.
Cleanup before connect or after failed token does not send left.
actualBefore: useCallEngine sent joined after connect but sent left only from the explicit leave handler; cleanup disconnected the room without backend left state.
fixApplied: Added joinedSessionIdRef set only after successful connect before joined state is posted.
Added idempotent markParticipantLeft() used by both explicit leave and cleanup/unmount.
Added unit tests with mocked LiveKit Room and call-client APIs.
redEvidence: New cleanup test failed before implementation: expected postParticipantState to be called 2 times, but got 1.
greenEvidence: cmd /c ..\..\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config vitest.config.ts src/lib/call/call-engine.test.tsx -> 4 tests passed.
limitations: This fixes only the participant-state cleanup bug found during media audit.

Full LiveKit/Jitsi provider verification remains unverified: provider config, prejoin, permissions, real join, remote participant, reconnect, leave/rejoin, and media-denied browser behavior still need live traversal.
regressionRisk: Medium-low. The change touches call teardown lifecycle; targeted tests cover explicit leave, cleanup after join, cleanup before connect, and failed provider token.
